rulings.Case Title: THE ESTATE OF WILLIAM SAYSON YAO
rulings.Calendar Matter: ACCOUNTING PETITION RE: Accounting #1, Report, Fees, Final Distribution
rulings.Rulings: Continued to 3/22/2021. Endorsed-filed single sided courtesy copies are required for any papers filed with the court and must be received by Examiner in room 202 at the Superior Court. Examiners will not review any document until after it has been filed and a courtesy copy has been received. [LRSF 2.7, 14.7]. The filing party must deliver courtesy copies to the Examiners no later than five (5) court days before the hearing with the scheduled hearing date noted on the face sheet and any cover letter. [LRSF 14.7 B.] The caption of the proposed order must begin with Order For Final Distribution. [LRSF 14.36 I. 6) b.]

rulings.Rulings: hearing. appearance required. issues are petition to remove PR and reinstate petitioner, and to declare Barbara Bartnof disinherited. the objections filed state no legal basis for removal is provided, and no contest clauses are only enforceable against direct contests as defined by the probate code, and Mr. Bartnof's claims do not meet that definition. no hearing notes will be sent. As a result of the renewed stay home order due to the COVID-19 emergency, effective 12/6/20, counsel and parties must, whenever possible, appear remotely through Zoom or CourtCall. Please review the 9th amended general order on the court's website for information on appearing remotely. For Zoom appearance: meeting ID is 875 8017 7925 and password is 722809. PARTIES APPEARING SHOULD CHECK IN FOR THE HEARING USING THEIR FULL NAME.

rulings.Case Title: THE ESTATE OF LYNN MCKANNAY
rulings.Calendar Matter: PETITION FOR LETTERS OF ADMINISTRATION, Appointment Under IAEA
rulings.Rulings: Continued to 3/22/2021. Issues: (Petitioner may want to seek the assistance of an attorney.) 1) An amended pleading or an amendment to a pleading requires the same notice of hearing (including publication) as the pleading it amends. [CRC 7.53]. 2) The Amended petition alleges on page 2 under #3. e.(3) all heirs at at law are adults and have waived bond. There are no waiver of bonds attached to the petition. "Amended pleading" means a pleading that completely restates and supersedes the pleading it amends for all purposes. [CRC 7.3] 3) Confidential Statement of Birth Date and Driver's License Number (Judicial Council Form DE-147S) is also required, pursuant to Probate Code 8404(b). Form DE-147S must be filed separately and confidentially. Do not file form DE-147S as an attachment to the Duties and Liabilities form. (This form cannot be e-filed). [LRSF 14.18].

rulings.Case Title: IN THE MATTER OF THE MARITSA CALDERON SPECIAL NEEDS TRUST
rulings.Calendar Matter: ACCOUNTING PETITION RE: Accounting #1, Report, Fees
rulings.Rulings: Hearing appearance required. Remote appearance by video or telephone should be made whenever possible. Please review the 9th amended general order for more information. To appear by Zoom, go to Zoom.com and click Join Meeting, and use the ID and password below. Meeting ID: 875 8017 7925 Password: 722809 To appear by telephone through Zoom, call 1-669-900-6833 and follow the prompts, using the meeting ID and password above. Parties appearing should check in for the hearing using their full name. ISSUES (may be addressed at hearing): 1) The account alleges $680,877.55 cash in a Citisavings Account. The FDIC insurance limit is $250,000.00. 2) On schedule C - Disbursements entry 12/05/2019 allege a disbursement for miscellaneous expenses totaling $3,720.37. Petitioner alleges $2,520.06 is for CBD medication to control seizures. The remaining $1,200.31 Petitioner alleges was for gas coffee/tea for a cross country vacation and the trust paid 1/3 of the expenses, Maritsa's portion. However, the receipts provided appear to show the trust paying the entire amount. 3) Petitioner amended the account schedules to begin 2/14/2019 but did not amend the information in the petition to reflect the 2/14/2019 account starting date.

rulings.Case Title: THE ESTATE OF STEVEN WAYNE RICKARDS
rulings.Calendar Matter: Petition For Order Authorizing Guardian Ad Litem Timothy P. Breen To Execute And File Disclaimers On Behalf Of Minors Lucas Craig Barber And Eliza Jane Kathryne Barber
rulings.Rulings: Hearing. Appearance required. The Court to determine if the guardian ad litem, Timothy P. Breen, should be authorized to execute and file disclaimers on behalf of the two minor heirs, Eliza Jane Kathryne Barber and Lucas Craig Barber. (No courtesy copies of the declarations filed after the last hearing have been received yet by this Examiner.) Due to the COVID-19 emergency, counsel and parties must, whenever possible, appear remotely through Zoom or CourtCall. Please review the 9th amended general order on the court's website for information on appearing remotely. For Zoom appearance: meeting ID is 875 8017 7925 and password is 722809. For audio-only telephone appearance via Zoom, call 1-669-900-6833 and follow the prompts, using the meeting ID and password above. PARTIES APPEARING SHOULD CHECK IN FOR THE HEARING USING THEIR FULL NAME.

rulings.Case Title: IN THE MATTER OF THE LEENA MARJUKKA HIETANEN REVOCABLE TRUST
rulings.Calendar Matter: Petition For Order Confirming Trust Asset
rulings.Rulings: Continued to 03/29/2021. Issues: 1) The Second Declaration was not responsive to the judicial officer's inquiry at the 12/07/2020 hearing about how the several other accounts (Ally, Aurora, Discover, Goldman Saches) were titled prior to being transfered into the account. At the hearing Mr. Peterson stated that he believes those accounts were personally titled, that if necessary, he could subpoena those records from the banks and requested a two-month continuance to do so.

rulings.Case Title: THE ESTATE OF BETTY J. HETT
rulings.Calendar Matter: PETITION FOR PROBATE OF WILL AND FOR LETTERS TESTAMENTARY
rulings.Rulings: Hearing. Appearance required. Court is to determine whether defects in notice should be waived and whether lost will should be admitted to probate. PARTIES APPEARING SHOULD CHECK IN FOR THE HEARING WITH THEIR FULL NAME. Counsel and parties must, whenever possible, appear remotely through Zoom or CourtCall. For a video appearance, go to zoom.us, click "join a meeting" and input meeting ID 875 8017 7925 and password 722809. For an audio-only appearance through Zoom, call 1-669-900-6833 and key in meeting ID "875 8017 7925#", then participant ID "0#", then password "722809#". (Toll rates may apply).) The Court prefers that counsel and parties appear by video and that they input their first and last name into the "Your Name" dialogue box. Counsel and parties may appear by audio-only telephone through CourtCall by calling 1-888-882-6878. Please review the Court's 9th Amended General Order which took effect on 02/10/2021 for more information.

rulings.Case Title: THE ESTATE OF RAY K. BERNSTEIN
rulings.Calendar Matter: PETITION FOR PROBATE OF WILL AND FOR LETTERS TESTAMENTARY
rulings.Rulings: Continued to 3/22/21. The Confidential Statement of Birth Date and Driver's License Number (Judicial Council Form DE-147S) is also required, pursuant to Probate Code Section 8404(b). Form DE-147S must be filed separately and confidentially. (New Rule as of January 2021.) When second generation or more distant heirs are listed, the deceased ancestor through whom they take (or would have taken) shall be named and his or her date of death and relationship to the decedent shall be stated. [LRSF 14.18 C.] Who were the parents of the predeceased uncle and aunt who was the common ancestor of the decedent? Courtesy copies of any responsive papers must be received by the assigned Examiner by no later than 3/11/21. [LRSF 2.7 B. and 14.7 B.]
